perturbation.original_text: On the basis of the representations and warranties herein contained and subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, the Company hereby appoints Sandler O'Neill as its Agent to consult with and advise the Company, and to assist the Company with the solicitation of subscriptions and purchase orders for Securities, in connection with the Company's sale of Common Stock in the Offerings. On the basis of the representations and warranties herein contained, and subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, Sandler O'Neill accepts such appointment and agrees to use its best efforts to assist the Company with the solicitation of subscriptions and purchase orders for Securities in accordance with this Agreement; provided, however, that the Agent shall not be obligated to take any action which is inconsistent with any applicable laws, regulations, decisions or orders.
perturbation.changed_text: On the basis of the representations and warranties herein contained and subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, the Company hereby appoints Sandler O'Neill as its Agent to consult with and advise the Company, and to assist the Company with the solicitation of subscriptions and purchase orders for Securities, in connection with the Company's sale of Common Stock in the Offerings. On the basis of the representations and warranties herein contained, and subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, Sandler O'Neill accepts such appointment but does not guarantee any specific outcome or result in assisting with the solicitation of subscriptions and purchase orders for Securities in accordance with this Agreement; provided, however, that the Agent shall not be obligated to take any action which is inconsistent with any applicable laws, regulations, decisions or orders.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding Sandler O'Neill's responsibility. Originally, they agree to use their 'best efforts,' suggesting a commitment to achieving a successful outcome. The modified text states they 'do not guarantee any specific outcome,' which contradicts the implicit expectation of 'best efforts' leading to a result. This creates an in-text contradiction if other sections of the agreement assume a level of performance aligned with a successful offering due to the agent’s best efforts, specifically under SECTION 2, where the agent's appointment is 'to assist the Company with the solicitation of subscriptions and purchase orders for Securities'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: On the basis of the representations and warranties herein contained and subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, the Company hereby appoints Sandler O'Neill as its Agent to consult with and advise the Company, and to assist the Company with the solicitation of subscriptions and purchase orders for Securities, in connection with the Company's sale of Common Stock in the Offerings. On the basis of the representations and warranties herein contained, and subject to the terms and conditions herein set forth, Sandler O'Neill accepts such appointment but does not guarantee any specific outcome or result in assisting with the solicitation of subscriptions and purchase orders for Securities in accordance with this Agreement; provided, however, that the Agent shall not be obligated to take any action which is inconsistent with any applicable laws, regulations, decisions or orders.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that Sandler O'Neill agrees to use its 'best efforts' to assist the Company with solicitation. The changed text adds that Sandler O'Neill 'does not guarantee any specific outcome'. While not explicitly contradictory on the surface, the addition undermines the implied expectation in the original text and creates an ambiguity regarding the agent's responsibility, especially given the agent's role 'to assist the Company with the solicitation of subscriptions and purchase orders for Securities'. By disclaiming a guaranteed outcome while still agreeing to use best efforts, it introduces uncertainty and can be interpreted as conflicting with the commitment level implied by 'best efforts' in the original and contradicted section, especially when their role is clearly defined in the contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Company will pay any stock issue and transfer taxes which may be payable with respect to the sale of the Securities. In addition to the reimbursement of the expenses specified in Section 4 hereof, the Agent will receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: (a) One percent (1.00%) of the aggregate purchase price of the Securities sold in the Subscription and Community Offering, excluding in each case shares purchased by (i) any employee benefit plan of the Company or the Bank established for the benefit of their respective directors, officers and employees, and (ii) any director, officer or employee of the Company or the Bank or members of their immediate families (which term shall mean parents, grandparents, spouse, siblings, children and grandchildren); and (b) With respect to any Securities sold by a National Association of Securities Dealers, Inc. ("NASD") member firm (other than Sandler O'Neill) in the Syndicated Community Offering, (i) the compensation payable to Selected Dealers, (ii) any sponsoring dealer's fees; and (iii) a management fee to Sandler O'Neill of one percent (1.0 %) of the aggregate purchase price of the Securities sold in the Syndicated Community Offering. Any fees payable to Sandler O'Neill for Securities sold by Sandler O'Neill under any such agreement shall be limited to an aggregate of six percent (6.0 %) of the purchase price of the Securities sold by Sandler O'Neill and other NASD member firms.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will pay any stock issue and transfer taxes which may be payable with respect to the sale of the Securities, except for any penalties incurred by delays. In addition to the reimbursement of the expenses specified in Section 4 hereof, the Agent *may* receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: (a) One percent (1.00%) of the aggregate purchase price of the Securities sold in the Subscription and Community Offering, excluding in each case shares purchased by (i) any employee benefit plan of the Company or the Bank established for the benefit of their respective directors, officers and employees, and (ii) any director, officer or employee of the Company or the Bank or members of their immediate families (which term shall mean parents, grandparents, spouse, siblings, children and grandchildren); and (b) With respect to any Securities sold by a National Association of Securities Dealers, Inc. ("NASD") member firm (other than Sandler O'Neill) in the Syndicated Community Offering, (i) the compensation payable to Selected Dealers, (ii) any sponsoring dealer's fees; and (iii) a management fee to Sandler O'Neill of one percent (1.0 %) of the aggregate purchase price of the Securities sold in the Syndicated Community Offering. Any fees payable to Sandler O'Neill for Securities sold by Sandler O'Neill under any such agreement shall be limited to an aggregate of six percent (6.0 %) of the purchase price of the Securities sold by Sandler O'Neill and other NASD member firms.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the Company pays the stock issue and transfer taxes but now contains an exception for 'penalties incurred by delays'. This creates an ambiguity regarding *who* is responsible for the delays that cause the penalties, and if the Agent is at fault, should the Company still cover this expense? Also by changing 'will receive' to 'may receive' introduces ambiguity about compensation. Initially, compensation is defined, however, by adding 'may', compensation becomes non-binding, especially if some clauses in SECTION 4 or 5 suggest that under certain performance conditions, the compensation structure defined here doesn't apply. These changes creates an internal contradiction and potential source of disputes concerning theAgent's payment conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company will pay any stock issue and transfer taxes which may be payable with respect to the sale of the Securities, except for any penalties incurred by delays. In addition to the reimbursement of the expenses specified in Section 4 hereof, the Agent *may* receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: (a) One percent (1.00%) of the aggregate purchase price of the Securities sold in the Subscription and Community Offering, excluding in each case shares purchased by (i) any employee benefit plan of the Company or the Bank established for the benefit of their respective directors, officers and employees, and (ii) any director, officer or employee of the Company or the Bank or members of their immediate families (which term shall mean parents, grandparents, spouse, siblings, children and grandchildren); and (b) With respect to any Securities sold by a National Association of Securities Dealers, Inc. ("NASD") member firm (other than Sandler O'Neill) in the Syndicated Community Offering, (i) the compensation payable to Selected Dealers, (ii) any sponsoring dealer's fees; and (iii) a management fee to Sandler O'Neill of one percent (1.0 %) of the aggregate purchase price of the Securities sold in the Syndicated Community Offering. Any fees payable to Sandler O'Neill for Securities sold by Sandler O'Neill under any such agreement shall be limited to an aggregate of six percent (6.0 %) of the purchase price of the Securities sold by Sandler O'Neill and other NASD member firms.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will pay' to 'except for any penalties incurred by delays' introduces ambiguity regarding the Company's responsibility for stock issue and transfer taxes. Specifically, the exception for penalties creates a conflict, as the contradicted text, which is the changed text in this case, now implies that the Company might *not* pay all such taxes. Additionally, changing 'will receive' to 'may receive' makes the Agent's compensation uncertain. This contradicts the initial implication of a guaranteed compensation structure, creating a potential source of disputes. Both these changes cause ambiguity in financial obligations, creating a strong internal contradiction according to the "Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction" definition.
